title: Fiery Croatian Mozzarella Flatbread
description: A quick, briny flatbread with melted mozzarella and a spicy kick of red pepper flakes, perfect for a lunch or snack.
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve 5g dry yeast in 100ml warm water with 1 tsp sugar and let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
In a mixing bowl, combine 200g flour, 1 tsp salt, and the yeast mixture; stir until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 20 minutes until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide it into four equal portions.
Roll each portion into a thin circle on a lightly floured surface about 10cm across.
Brush each flatbread with a thin layer of olive oil and sprinkle evenly with 1 tbsp red pepper flakes.
Top each flatbread with 50g sliced mozzarella and a spoonful of tomato sauce.
Fold each flatbread in half and press the edges to seal.
Preheat a cast-iron skillet over medium heat and cook the folded flatbreads for 3 minutes on each side until golden and cheese is melted.
Remove from skillet, garnish with chopped basil, and drizzle a pinch of sea salt.
